content.title: Buff Wash & Exposed Aggregate Concrete
content.tagline: The Finish Made to Be Touched
content.subtitle: The Finish Made to Be Touched
content.description: A surface wash that reveals the natural stone embedded in the mix, giving every pour a rich, organic texture you can both see and feel.

content.title: The Concrete Finish Made to Be Touched
content.description: Buff Wash and Exposed Aggregate finishes elevate standard concrete into tactile, visually rich surfaces that celebrate texture, color, and natural beauty. By using specialized chemical techniques, Maxima Concrete transforms plain slabs into stunning, slip-resistant surfaces — perfect for patios, pool decks, walkways, and entryways.

content.title: Crafting Beauty Beneath the Surface
content.description: These finishes begin with freshly poured, high-quality concrete. A chemical surface retarder is applied to slow down the curing of the topmost “cream” layer. After a controlled period, our team carefully removes this thin layer using a high-pressure washer, revealing the fine sands and stones hidden within the concrete. The result is a naturally slip-resistant, multi-textured surface that’s as beautiful as it is durable.

items.question: What's the difference between buff wash and exposed aggregate?
items.answer: Both reveal the decorative stone within the concrete, but buff wash removes only the top surface for a subtle, sandy texture, while exposed aggregate exposes more stone for a bolder, pebbled look.

items.question: Is exposed aggregate slip-resistant?
items.answer: Yes. The textured surface provides excellent traction, making it a popular, safe choice for pool decks, patios, and walkways.

items.question: How do I maintain a buff wash or exposed aggregate surface?
items.answer: Simple cleaning and resealing every 2–3 years keeps the finish vibrant and protects it from stains and weather.

items.question: Can I choose the color and stone?
items.answer: Absolutely. We offer a range of aggregate types and integral colors so your finish complements your home and landscape.
